en_body:
I want to make a love poem, very simple and easy to understand; without a touch of cleverness or polish:
 To tell You—and anyone who wishes to listen together—the story of my heart:
Using simple words, such as:
 I think of You...making:
 The day wide, the month long; the road far over mountains, seas; the night deep, Love deeper
 I watch and wait!
I want to describe:
 When I think of You: the color of night cradling; the color of stars so clear
 Love ascends the throne: the heart's secret leaves me bewildered!
I often sit in silence, looking upward and feeling these thoughts
 So true-hearted: making me become more real
 No longer too vague within the realm of Imagination!
I am happy even when I cry from missing You:
 Because the love I give You is honest and straight
 Making me tremble with true sincerity
 And therefore I become too real!
I want to tell:
 Sometimes I only sit and taste the salt of tears
 Brimming from the corners of my eyes then rolling to my lips—my heart so tender!
And finally I see I need only carefully form three words,
 So gentle yet deep, yet profound:
 "I love You!"
And truthfully confess: I am waiting, also for three words
 "You love Me!"
note: The capitalized You and Me preserve the Vietnamese intimate pronouns “Anh” and “Em,” which carry the lovers’ gendered relationship.
